sections.title: Trying to search the specimen tray without a specimen jar

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Oi! What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on an errand.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Oh yeah? And whose errand is that then? Where is your specimen jar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I don't have one.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: And you reckon you have been sent on an errand - without a specimen jar? Sorry, I can't let you do that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am searching this tray.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Oh you are, are you? Well where's your specimen jar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... I don't have one.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Well how can you handle the specimens without it? As you should know, specimens are to be kept in specimen jars to keep them safe and preserved. Next time bring it along!

sections.title: Trying to use the western winch before getting permission

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Sorry; this area is private. The only way you'll get to use these is by impressing the archaeological expert up at the centre.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Find something worthwhile and he might let you use the winches. Until then, get lost!

sections.title: Digging with a spade in soil

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Oi! What do you think you're doing? There's fragile specimens around here!

sections.title: Using a specimen brush on soil

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Use your brush on specimens to clean them, not the dirt!

sections.title: Using a trowel on soil in a level 1 site without wearing leather gloves

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Hey, where are your leather gloves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... I haven't got any.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Well get some and put them on first!

sections.title: Using a trowel on soil in a level 1 site while wearing leather gloves but not leather boots

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Oi! No boots! No leather boots: no digging!

sections.title: Using a trowel on soil in a level 2 site

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Sorry, you must use a rock pick to dig at a level 2 site.

sections.title: Using a trowel on the specimen tray

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Excuse me! No digging in the specimen trays please.

sections.title: Using a rock pick on a level 1 or 3 site

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: No, no; rock picks should only be used to dig at a level 2 site.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Good day; what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: I am involved in various stages of the dig, from the initial investigation to the installation of the mine shafts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure...

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Well, let me know when you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I dig around here?

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: You can only use a site you have the appropriate exam level for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Appropriate exam level?

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Yes, only persons with the correct Earth Sciences certificate can dig here. A level 1 certificate will let you dig in a level 1 site and so on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, yes; I understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am already skilled in digging.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Well that's nice for you. You can't dig around here without a certificate though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: Ah, it's the great archaeologist! Congratulations on your discovery.

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: What do you think you're doing?
